title: Fix internvl fp16 error

step_plan.summary: Make the InternVL attention safe for fp16/XPU by ensuring the query tensor is contiguous before calling scaled_dot_product_attention and replacing direct torch.softmax usage with the project's attention_softmax helper (imported from common.py). This is a minimal internal fix with no public API changes.

steps.operation: Make query contiguous before SDP and use attention_softmax in non-SDP path (import helper)
steps.reason: On fp16/XPU targets, non-contiguous query tensors can trigger low-level kernel/runtime errors when passed to scaled_dot_product_attention. The code already makes k and v contiguous but leaves q possibly non-contiguous. Also, direct use of torch.softmax on fp16/XPU tensors can be numerically or kernel-unsafe. The project provides attention_softmax in common.py which handles XPU/fp16 safely (including dtype promotion when needed). Passing q.contiguous() into the SDP path and replacing torch.softmax with attention_softmax in the non-SDP path applies the minimal, targeted fixes to avoid fp16 errors.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral/API: No public API changes. Implementation-level: q.contiguous() may introduce an additional memory copy if q was non-contiguous, causing a small runtime and memory overhead; could slightly change performance characteristics of attention (measurements recommended). Tests: Run fp16 and XPU-specific unit/smoke tests for InternVL and attention codepaths; existing tests that rely on specific memory layouts should still pass but may reveal assumptions about contiguity. Dependents: Any other code calling intern_attention_forward is unaffected functionally; however, if callers depended on q remaining non-contiguous for zero-copy semantics (unlikely), their performance profile could change. Deployment: No configuration changes required.
